ref: A little while later some people standing there walked over to Peter and said, “We know you are one of them. We can tell it because you talk like someone from Galilee.”
text: Ti la iŋɛ fii wa ti nɛreba la n ze' la paaɛ Pita zi'an yele en yeti, “Yelemɛŋerɛ fum dela ba ayima. Fum tugum n pa'alɛ.”
translated: That's why when the people saw that Peter was standing at the door, they said, “You are also one of them.” His accent betrayed him.

ref: When Herod found out that the wise men from the east had tricked him, he was very angry. He gave orders for his men to kill all the boys who lived in or near Bethlehem and were two years old and younger. This was based on what he had learned from the wise men.
text: Hɛrɔd n ta baŋɛ ti baŋereba la pã'ahɛ en mɛ la, a ukɛ mɛ kankaŋi, gee bo' nuurɛ ti ba doohe kɔrum bibudibeto sɛba woo n boi Bɛtelehɛm la ka kɔrɔmkɔrɔm za'a, kɔmsɛba n de yuuma ayi' bii sɛba n pun ka paaɛ bala, doohe la hansɛka ti a wum ti baŋereba duma la tɔgɛ bo' en la.
translated: When Herod realized that the wise men had outwitted him, he was furious, and he gave orders to kill all the boys in Bethlehem and its vicinity who were two years old or younger, based on the time he had learned from the wise men.

ref: If I use the power of Beelzebul to force out demons, whose power do your own followers use to force them out? Your followers are the ones who will judge you.
text: Bala, mam han tara Beelezebule sa'aha kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi, gee yamam nɛreba la me tari la ani sa'aha kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi? Bala zuo, ya mɛhi zahereba la en wan gakɛ ya.
translated: Therefore, if Beelzebub drives out demons by the power of the prince of demons, then by whose power do your people drive them out? So then, they will be your judges.

ref: Many false prophets will come and fool a lot of people.

ref: It will be a terrible time for women who are expecting babies or nursing young children.
text: Yele boi pɔgepuuhi, la sɛba en tari kɔma ti ba mɔgera hankaŋa puan la.
translated: The child is very stubborn, and if you scold him he will cry.

ref: when four people came up, carrying a man on a mat because he could not walk.
text: Bala ma'a ti nɛreba banaahi zɛ kɔɔreŋɔ wa'am Yezu zi'an na.
translated: The people who were there heard him and believed in Jesus.

ref: When Jesus heard that John had been put in prison, he went to Galilee.
text: Yezu en ta wum ti ba nyɔkɛ Yɔn pagɛ mɛ la, a yehe mɛ kiŋɛ Galilii.
translated: And it came to pass, as Jesus was walking by the sea of Galilee, he saw two brothers.

ref: Jesus told his disciples to tell the people to sit down on the green grass.
text: Ti a bo' ba nuurɛ ti ba bahɛ ti nɛreba la tigehe taaba zi'ire muɔ la zuo.
translated: They are the ones who are causing problems, they are the ones who are spoiling our good name.

ref: If a soldier forces you to carry his pack one kilometer, carry it two kilometers.
text: Se'em han pɛregɛ fum ti fum kiŋɛ miilɛ deyina la en, kiŋɛ miilɛ pa'ahɛ en.
translated: If you want to go to Fum, go to Fum with a lot of money, with a lot of money.
